text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY -- CLINICAL TRIAL FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Phase I/II KRAS G12D Inhibitor Trial -- Protocol Assessment Visit (Cycle 3, Day 1)

---

ATTENDING ONCOLOGIST: [Protocol Investigator, Medical Oncology]
CLINICAL TRIAL UNIT: Regional Cancer Center, Phase I/II Program
VISIT TYPE: Protocol-mandated tumor assessment and safety evaluation
PROTOCOL VISIT: Cycle 3, Day 1 (on-study safety and early efficacy assessment)

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Mr. Gerald Holloway is a 59-year-old male with metastatic sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma (KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS, BRAF wild-type) presenting for his Cycle 3, Day 1 protocol visit on the Phase I/II trial evaluating an investigational oral KRAS G12D-selective inhibitor. This visit includes protocol-mandated safety labs, clinical assessment, toxicity grading, and early tumor marker review. Full restaging CT imaging is anticipated at the end of cycle 4 per protocol schedule.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr. Holloway has a well-characterized history of Stage IVA (AJCC 8th edition) sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma with synchronous hepatic metastases, diagnosed approximately 21 months ago. His disease course has been as follows:

He was initially diagnosed at age 58 with a 4.8 cm sigmoid colon primary with four synchronous liver metastases (largest 3.1 cm in segment VI). Molecular profiling at diagnosis revealed KRAS p.G12D mutation (VAF 41%), concurrent APC frameshift and TP53 R175H gain-of-function hotspot mutations, MSS status by both IHC and NGS, TMB 6 mut/Mb, and BRAF V600E wild-type. HER2 was not amplified. These features rendered him ineligible for anti-EGFR therapy or immune checkpoint inhibition.

He underwent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy (R0 resection, pT3 pN2a, 3/18 nodes positive, LVI and PNI present) followed by first-line mFOLFOX6 plus bev. He had a meaningful partial response -- all four liver lesions shrank substantially -- but was limited by cumulative oxali-related peripheral sensory neuropathy, which progressed from grade 1 to grade 2-3 over 10 cycles, requiring early Eloxatin discontinuation. He transitioned to maintenance 5-FU/leucovorin plus Avastin, but the disease progressed at approximately 10 months from treatment start, with growth of the hepatic lesions and emergence of two new right-sided pulmonary nodules. His CEA had risen to 41 ng/mL at that time from a nadir of 22 ng/mL.

He was then treated with second-line FOLFIRI plus ramu. He had an initial partial response (CEA down to 22 ng/mL, hepatic lesion shrinkage), with disease stabilized for roughly 6 months, but progressed again at approximately 19 months from initial diagnosis -- hepatic lesion regrowth and pulmonary nodule enlargement, CEA 58 ng/mL. He had notable treatment-related hypertension throughout his anti-angiogenic therapy course, ultimately requiring triple antihypertensive therapy (lisinopril 20 mg, amlodipine 10 mg, hydrochlorothiazide 12.5 mg).

Given his confirmed KRAS G12D-driven disease (re-biopsy of the right lower lobe pulmonary nodule prior to trial entry confirmed metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma, KRAS G12D VAF 38%, MSS, no NRAS/BRAF resistance mutations, no KRAS amplification), he was enrolled approximately 1.5 months ago onto the current Phase I/II trial of an investigational oral KRAS G12D-selective inhibitor. He is at the assigned dose cohort per protocol. Today he presents for his Cycle 3, Day 1 assessment, completing approximately 6 weeks on the investigational agent.

Since initiating the trial drug, Mr. Holloway reports feeling subjectively somewhat better. He has noticed a decrease in his abdominal discomfort. He endorses some ongoing mild nausea, predominantly in the first 1-2 hours after taking the morning dose, and notes a mild acneiform rash across his face and upper chest, which he has been treating with topical clindamycin gel as directed. He denies vomiting, significant diarrhea, dyspnea, hemoptysis, chest pain, new bone pain, headache, or visual changes. He denies any bleeding events. He is taking his investigational agent as prescribed and reports no missed doses. He is eating reasonably well, noting improved appetite compared to his last months on FOLFIRI/ramu.

His residual peripheral neuropathy from prior oxali has been gradually but incompletely improving. He continues to notice persistent numbness in both feet, particularly on uneven ground, but this has not worsened and is not causing him falls or significant functional limitation at this time.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: Mild fatigue, grade 1. Improved appetite. No fever, chills, or night sweats. No unintentional weight loss recently (weight stable over last 4 weeks).
- HEENT: Mild acneiform rash on face and upper chest, grade 1. No oral ulcers or significant mucositis currently. No vision changes.
- Cardiovascular: No chest pain, no palpitations, no exertional dyspnea. Hypertension -- being managed as below. No lower extremity edema.
- Respiratory: No cough, dyspnea, hemoptysis. Pulmonary nodules known and under surveillance.
- GI: Mild nausea (grade 1), occurring primarily post-dose in the morning. No vomiting. Bowel habits essentially normal (1-2 formed stools per day). No hematochezia. Mild intermittent right-upper-quadrant discomfort attributed to hepatic disease, unchanged from prior visits.
- GU: No dysuria, no hematuria. Adequate urine output.
- Neurologic: Bilateral foot numbness (grade 1 residual oxali-related sensory neuropathy). No new neurologic symptoms. No headaches.
- Musculoskeletal: Mild diffuse fatigue; no new joint pain or bone pain.
- Skin: Grade 1 acneiform/papulopustular rash -- face, forehead, upper chest -- appearing after starting the investigational agent; being treated with topical clindamycin 1% gel applied twice daily. No significant pruritus. No desquamation.
- Hematologic: No bruising, no bleeding, no petechiae.
- Endocrine: No symptoms suggestive of hypothyroidism or adrenal insufficiency.
- Psychiatric: Reports mild anxiety regarding disease and trial participation; in good spirits today, appreciating the possibility of benefit. Adequate sleep.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1. Metastatic sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma, KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS -- stage IVA at diagnosis (synchronous hepatic metastases; subsequently pulmonary metastases at progression)
2. Hypertension -- now on triple therapy as above; initially well-controlled on lisinopril alone prior to anti-angiogenic therapy
3. Hyperlipidemia -- on atorvastatin
4. Eloxatin-induced peripheral sensory neuropathy (cumulative from first-line therapy) -- grade 1 residual

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with primary anastomosis (low anterior resection approach), R0 resection, pT3 pN2a
- CT-guided percutaneous biopsy of right lower lobe pulmonary nodule (pre-trial re-biopsy)

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No family history of colorectal cancer, Lynch syndrome, or other hereditary gastrointestinal malignancies
- Father: coronary artery disease
- Mother: type 2 diabetes mellitus

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker: quit approximately 14 years ago; 20 pack-year history. No current tobacco use
- Alcohol: occasional, less than 1 drink per week
- Occupation: civil engineer (currently on medical leave)
- Lives with spouse; supportive family. Independently ambulatory
- No illicit drug use

ALLERGIES:
- No known drug allergies

MEDICATIONS:
1. Investigational KRAS G12D inhibitor (oral, protocol-assigned dose) -- twice daily with food per protocol
2. Lisinopril 20 mg orally daily
3. Amlodipine 10 mg orally daily
4. Hydrochlorothiazide 12.5 mg orally daily
5. Atorvastatin 40 mg orally nightly
6. Ondansetron 8 mg orally as needed (nausea, pre-dose prophylaxis)
7. Loperamide 2 mg orally as needed (diarrhea management per prior regimen; using rarely)
8. Topical clindamycin 1% gel -- applied to face and upper chest twice daily (for rash)
9. Acetaminophen 500-1000 mg as needed (infrequent)

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Alert and oriented x3, in no acute distress. Well-appearing, mildly fatigued affect. Cooperative and conversant.
Vital Signs: Blood pressure 135/84 mmHg (left arm, seated), heart rate 74 bpm and regular, respiratory rate 16 breaths/min, temperature 36.7 degrees C (oral), SpO2 97% on room air, weight 81.4 kg (stable from prior visit, +0.4 kg).
Performance Status: ECOG 1 (ambulatory, capable of self-care, some restriction in physically strenuous activity).
Skin: Grade 1 acneiform eruption involving bilateral cheeks, forehead, and upper chest -- scattered erythematous papules and pustules, no significant crusting or desquamation. No signs of secondary infection. No jaundice or significant pallor.
HEENT: Anicteric sclerae. Oropharynx clear, no mucositis or oral lesions. No lymphadenopathy in the cervical or submandibular chains.
Chest/Respiratory: Lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezing, no crackles, no pleural rub. No accessory muscle use.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1 and S2. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs. No jugular venous distension. No peripheral edema.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Mild tenderness to deep palpation in the right upper quadrant without peritoneal signs. No guarding. Well-healed lower midline and port-site laparoscopic scars from prior colectomy. Liver edge not grossly palpable below the costal margin. No splenomegaly. No ascites appreciated.
Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Upper extremity strength and sensation grossly intact. Lower extremity: bilateral sock-and-stocking distribution diminished light touch sensation in feet (grade 1 residual neuropathy), consistent with prior oxali toxicity. Gait: antalgic but steady on flat surface.
Extremities: No clubbing, cyanosis, or significant edema.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (PROTOCOL-REQUIRED, TODAY'S VISIT):
Complete Blood Count:
- WBC: 5.1 x10^3/uL (normal)
- ANC: 3,200/uL (adequate)
- Hemoglobin: 11.2 g/dL (mild anemia, slightly improved from 10.8 g/dL at prior cycle)
- Platelets: 214 x10^3/uL (normal)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium: 138 mEq/L, Potassium: 3.9 mEq/L, Chloride: 101 mEq/L, Bicarbonate: 24 mEq/L
- BUN: 17 mg/dL, Creatinine: 0.98 mg/dL (eGFR >60 mL/min/1.73 m2, acceptable for protocol)
- Glucose: 102 mg/dL (mildly elevated, not clinically significant)
- Total bilirubin: 0.9 mg/dL (normal)
- AST: 41 U/L (mildly elevated, within protocol-acceptable range; slightly improved from prior 48 U/L)
- ALT: 46 U/L (mildly elevated, within protocol-acceptable range; slightly improved from prior 52 U/L)
- Alkaline phosphatase: 88 U/L (normal)
- Albumin: 3.7 g/dL (slightly low-normal; adequate)

Coagulation (as required per protocol):
- PT/INR: 1.1 (normal)

Tumor Markers:
- CEA: 42 ng/mL (down from 58 ng/mL at trial entry approximately 6 weeks ago; this represents a 28% decline in CEA from baseline)
- CA 19-9: 35 U/mL (down from 47 U/mL at trial entry; 26% decline)

Thyroid function (protocol-required baseline and cycle 3 check):
- TSH: 2.3 mIU/L (normal)

Urinalysis (protocol-required):
- Protein: trace (no significant proteinuria; acceptable for anti-angiogenic-experienced patient)
- No hematuria

---

IMAGING:
Full restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis is scheduled at the end of Cycle 4 per protocol (approximately 4 weeks from today). No new imaging obtained at this visit. The most recent available imaging dates from approximately 2 months prior (the pre-trial staging CT demonstrating progression on FOLFIRI/Cyramza), which showed: segment VI hepatic lesion 1.5 cm, segment IVb 1.2 cm, segment II 0.6 cm, segment VII 0.4 cm; right lower lobe pulmonary nodule 0.9 cm; right upper lobe 0.5 cm; CEA 58 ng/mL at that time.

The patient will return for formal radiographic restaging at the Cycle 5, Day 1 visit.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

1. METASTATIC SIGMOID COLON ADENOCARCINOMA, KRAS G12D-MUTANT, MSS, BRAF WILD-TYPE -- THIRD-LINE TREATMENT ON INVESTIGATIONAL KRAS G12D INHIBITOR TRIAL:

Mr. Holloway is a 59-year-old male with heavily pretreated metastatic colorectal cancer (two prior lines of systemic therapy) who has now completed approximately 6 weeks (two full cycles plus lead-in to Cycle 3) on the investigational KRAS G12D-selective inhibitor. He has tolerated the drug reasonably well with only low-grade toxicities (grade 1 nausea, grade 1 rash, grade 1 fatigue). Critically, tumor markers are showing an encouraging early decline: CEA has fallen from 58 ng/mL at trial entry to 42 ng/mL today (approximately 28% decrease), and CA 19-9 has dropped from 47 to 35 U/mL. While we cannot draw firm conclusions from marker kinetics alone, this directionally favorable trend is the first marker improvement since his progression on FOLFIRI/Cyramza and is consistent with possible early biological activity of the investigational agent.

Formal radiographic restaging is planned at the end of Cycle 4, approximately 4 weeks from today, which will provide the first objective tumor response assessment on the current protocol. The patient is aware that marker response does not guarantee radiographic benefit, but his cautious optimism is appropriate and his motivation remains strong.

All protocol-required assessments completed. No dose-limiting toxicities identified this cycle. Patient will continue at the current protocol-assigned dose. Dose modification criteria per protocol reviewed -- no modifications warranted at this time.

Patient will proceed to Cycle 3 dosing today after completion of protocol assessments.

2. TOXICITY MANAGEMENT:

a. Acneiform/papulopustular rash, grade 1 (face and upper chest): This pattern of rash is notable in the context of KRAS pathway-targeted agents and is not entirely unexpected given the downstream effects on MAPK/ERK signaling. It is currently grade 1 and not limiting. He is already applying topical clindamycin 1% gel twice daily with reasonable control. Counseled to avoid irritating skincare products and to use a gentle moisturizer. Sunscreen recommended (SPF 30 or higher) given photosensitivity risk. If rash progresses to grade 2 (covering more than 10% BSA or associated with significant discomfort), we will escalate to oral doxycycline 100 mg twice daily per the rash management algorithm. Protocol team dermatologist available for consultation if needed. Rash graded as CTCAE grade 1; no dose modification required.

b. Nausea, grade 1: Occurring primarily in the first 1-2 hours after the morning dose. Counseled to take the investigational agent with a small snack or light meal and to use ondansetron 8 mg pre-dose as needed. No vomiting reported. Adequate oral intake maintained. CTCAE grade 1; no dose modification warranted.

c. Fatigue, grade 1: Mild, consistent with disease burden and ongoing oncologic treatment. No specific intervention beyond counseling regarding pacing, light activity as tolerated, and optimization of sleep hygiene. CTCAE grade 1.

d. Residual peripheral sensory neuropathy, grade 1 (bilateral feet, from prior Eloxatin): Stable, not worsening. This is expected to be unrelated to the investigational KRAS G12D inhibitor. Patient continues with supportive measures; no pharmacologic intervention currently warranted. He was advised to continue wearing supportive footwear and to avoid high-risk environments for falls.

e. Hypertension: Blood pressure 135/84 mmHg today -- excellent control on current triple antihypertensive regimen (lisinopril 20 mg, amlodipine 10 mg, HCTZ 12.5 mg). His prior episodes of anti-angiogenic-related grade 3 hypertension were managed with the addition of these agents, and his blood pressure has been stable now for several visits. Continuing current antihypertensive regimen without change. Home BP monitoring reinforced. CTCAE grade 1 at today's visit.

f. Mild anemia: Hemoglobin 11.2 g/dL, slightly improved from 10.8 g/dL at prior cycle. Likely anemia of chronic disease in the context of ongoing malignancy. No iron deficiency pattern on prior labs. Monitoring; no erythropoietic agent or transfusion indicated at this time.

g. Mildly elevated transaminases (AST 41, ALT 46): Slightly improved from prior visit. Likely related to hepatic metastatic burden; within protocol-acceptable range. Trend will be monitored. No additional hepatotoxicity workup indicated at this time given stable/mildly improving pattern.

3. HYPERTENSION -- ONGOING MANAGEMENT:
As noted above, blood pressure is well-controlled. Continuing current triple regimen. Patient instructed to call the clinic for any home readings above 155/100 mmHg. Nephrology consultation not needed at this time.

4. HYPERLIPIDEMIA:
Continuing atorvastatin 40 mg nightly. No new lipid-related concerns.

5. GOALS OF CARE / PATIENT COUNSELING:
Mr. Holloway and his spouse were present for today's visit. He remains fully informed of his diagnosis, treatment status, and clinical trajectory. He has previously completed a goals of care conversation and has expressed his desire to continue active cancer-directed therapy as long as tolerable and potentially beneficial options remain. He understands the investigational nature of the current protocol. We discussed that the marker response is encouraging but that objective radiographic data is the critical determinant of whether the current regimen is providing meaningful benefit. He expressed understanding and appreciation for this framing. We reviewed warning signs necessitating urgent contact: fever above 38.3 degrees C, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting, severe diarrhea (greater than 6 stools per day), worsening rash with blistering or mucous membrane involvement, severe headache or visual changes, shortness of breath at rest, chest pain, significant bleeding, or any sudden neurologic change.

6. PERFORMANCE STATUS:
ECOG PS 1 today. Improved from ECOG 1-2 at the time of trial enrollment. He is ambulatory and independent with ADLs. His quality of life per patient report is improved compared to the last months of FOLFIRI/Cyramza, which he attributed in part to the absence of IV infusions and cumulative chemotherapy side effects.

---

PLAN SUMMARY:
- Cycle 3 of investigational KRAS G12D inhibitor to proceed today at current protocol dose
- Protocol-required safety and efficacy labs completed (CBC, CMP, LFTs, coagulation, TSH, CEA, CA 19-9, UA)
- Continue topical clindamycin 1% gel for grade 1 acneiform rash; sunscreen counseling
- Continue ondansetron as needed for grade 1 nausea; take drug with food
- Continue antihypertensive triple regimen; home BP monitoring
- Continue atorvastatin
- Return for Cycle 4, Day 1 in approximately 2 weeks; restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis to be scheduled for the end of Cycle 4 per protocol
- Continue supportive care for residual grade 1 peripheral neuropathy
- Patient to call clinic for any protocol-defined toxicity concerns or new symptoms between visits
- Cancer present; early tumor marker response noted on investigational KRAS G12D inhibitor therapy; objective radiographic assessment pending at Cycle 5, Day 1

---

Time spent: 45 minutes (face-to-face encounter, including clinical assessment, protocol documentation, and patient/family counseling). Visit complexity: high.

Electronically signed by: [Protocol Investigator, Medical Oncology, Phase I/II Clinical Trial Unit, Regional Cancer Center]
